info.title: Sparklink Dating API
info.description:
Smart Links, Real Connections — Full API for the Sparklink mobile application.

**Suspension:** Every protected endpoint (Bearer token required) may return a `403` if the account has been suspended. The response includes `suspended_until` so the app can show the exact time the ban lifts.

```json
{ "status": false, "message": "Your account has been suspended until ...", "data": [{ "suspended_until": "2026-04-23T10:00:00.000Z" }] }
```

Suspended.description: Account suspended — returned by the auth middleware on every protected endpoint when the user has been reported and suspended for 24 hours.

example.message: Your account has been suspended until Wed, 23 Apr 2026 10:00:00 GMT due to a report filed against you.

tags.name: Availability
tags.description: User availability for video call scheduling

tags.name: Rewards
tags.description: Sparklink Club rewards and QR offers

tags.name: Static
tags.description: Static content pages — Privacy Policy, Terms & Conditions, About

post.description: Creates a new unverified account and sends a 6-digit OTP to the email. The account cannot be used until the OTP is verified via `POST /auth/verify-email`. `terms_accepted` must be `true` — corresponds to the checkbox on the signup screen.

terms_accepted.description: User must accept Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy before registering

400.description: terms_accepted is false/missing, or passwords do not match

post.summary: Resend verification OTP — screen 07 countdown reset

post.description: Returns a 7-day JWT on success. Include it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all protected endpoints. Returns 403 if the email has not been verified yet.
Stateless logout — the server returns success and the client must discard the JWT. JWTs are not blocklisted server-side; the token simply expires after 7 days.

get.summary: Get current user full profile (photos, interests, location, preferences)
get.description: Returns the complete profile of the logged-in user including uploaded photos, selected interests, location, and preferences. Used on the Edit Profile screen and to resume onboarding from the correct step via `current_step`.

put.summary: Update profile — all onboarding + edit profile fields — screen 49

post.summary: Upload up to 5 profile photos — screens 11-12 (multipart/form-data)

200.description: Photos uploaded. Returns array of URLs.

get.summary: Get all interest tags with icons — screen 16 (public)
Get all 25 questions with category and options — screens 18-42 (public)

200.description: Questions array — each has question_id, text, type, category_name, options[]

answer.description: For scale questions — numeric string e.g. "3"

get.summary: Get a user answers

200.description: User answers with question text and option text

get.summary: Get all confirmed matches for current user
get.description: Returns all mutual matches (status = matched) for the logged-in user. Each record includes `match_id` which is required for `GET /api/matches/{matchId}/profile` and `POST /api/video-calls`.

200.description: Matches list — each includes match_id, matched_user_id

get.summary: Get potential matches — screen 44
get.description:
Returns all verified users matching the caller's gender/age/distance preferences, excluding only blocked users. Each candidate carries an `interaction_status` flag so Flutter can render the correct heart state without a second API call.

**interaction_status values:**
- `null` — no interaction yet
- `i_liked` — caller already liked them
- `i_superliked` — caller already superliked them
- `i_disliked` — caller already disliked them
- `they_liked` — they liked the caller (show indicator)
- `matched` — mutual match confirmed

**match_id** is non-null only when `interaction_status === 'matched'` — pass it to `GET /api/matches/{matchId}/profile`.

**has_my_availability** / **has_their_availability** — booleans indicating whether each side has set recurring time slots. Both must be true before a video call can be scheduled.

200.description: Candidates sorted: they_liked → null → liked/superliked → matched → disliked, then by match_percentage desc

400.description: Profile, preferences, or location not yet completed

post.description: Records a like in the swipes table. If the target user has already liked the caller, a mutual match is created automatically (status = matched) and both users receive a push notification. The response contains `result: 'liked'` or `result: 'matched'`.
Same as like but records action as `superlike`. The target user's notification will show a superlike indicator. Auto-creates a match if mutual.
Records a dislike in the swipes table. The disliked user remains visible in potential matches with `interaction_status: 'i_disliked'` so the caller can change their mind later.
Ends a confirmed match. The match record status becomes `unmatched` and neither user will appear in each other's matched list. The caller must be a participant of the match.
Sets match status to `blocked`. The blocked user is excluded from all future potential match results for the caller and vice versa.

get.summary: Full profile of matched user with compatibility analysis — screens 50-51
get.description: Returns the full profile of the other participant in a confirmed match, including photos, interests, match percentage, gap report, and match reasons. Only accessible to the two participants of the match.

200.description: user + photos + interests + match_percentage + gap_report + match_reasons

get.summary: Get phone and Instagram of matched user — screen 55 (It is a Match!)
get.description: Returns the private contact details (phone number and Instagram username) of the matched user. Only revealed after a confirmed mutual match. Only accessible to the two match participants.

get.summary: Get my availability slots — screen 52
get.description: Returns all recurring weekly time slots the caller has set. Each slot has an `id` that can be passed to `POST /api/video-calls` as `availability_id` when scheduling a call.

200.description: Array of { id, day_of_week, start_time, end_time }

post.summary: Set my availability — screen 52 (replaces all existing slots)
post.description: Replaces the caller's entire availability schedule with the provided slots. Send an empty array to clear all slots.

post.summary: Request the other user to set their availability
post.description:
Sends a push notification to the other match participant asking them to add their available time slots. This is shown when `has_their_availability` is `false` on the potential matches or match profile screen.

**Limits:**
- Maximum **3 requests** can be sent per match per requester. On the 3rd send the response still succeeds but `requests_remaining` will be `0`.
- A 4th attempt returns HTTP 429.
- If the other user has already set their availability, returns HTTP 400 (no notification sent).

**Notification sent to the other user:**
> "Sophie wants to schedule a video date with you! Please add your available times so you can connect."

The notification `reference_id` is the requester's `user_id` — tap navigates to their profile.

parameters.name: matchId
parameters.description: The match_id of the confirmed match

example.message: Request sent. You have 2 requests remaining.

400.description: Other user already has availability set — no notification needed

403.description: Caller is not a participant of this match

429.description: 3-request limit already reached for this match

example.message: You can only send 3 availability requests per match. Limit reached.

get.summary: Get overlapping time slots with a match — screens 52-53
get.description: Computes recurring weekly time windows where both matched users are available. Each result includes `availability_id` (the caller's slot ID) — pass this directly to `POST /api/video-calls` to schedule the call. An empty array means the users have no overlapping slots (show screen 53 no-overlap state). Add `?day=Mon` to filter by a specific weekday.

parameters.name: matchId
parameters.description: The match_id from the matches table

parameters.name: day
parameters.description: Optional filter — omit to return all overlapping days

403.description: Not authorized — you must be a participant of this match

get.summary: Get a matched user's availability — screen 52 match side
get.description: Returns the recurring weekly availability slots of another user. Requires an active confirmed match between the caller and the target user — returns 403 otherwise.

post.summary: Schedule a video call — screen 52 (Schedule Call button)
post.description:
Schedules a video call between two matched users. The caller picks an overlapping availability slot from `GET /api/availability/overlap/{matchId}` and passes its `availability_id` here. The backend verifies that both participants have matching availability on that day, then computes the next real-world occurrence of that recurring time slot as `scheduled_time`.

**Flow:**
1. Call `GET /api/availability/overlap/{matchId}` — get overlapping slots
2. User taps a slot → its `availability_id` is sent here
3. Backend validates overlap, creates the Agora channel, and returns RTC credentials
4. Both users call this endpoint with the **same** `match_id` — the second caller re-uses the existing channel and receives their own RTC token
5. A push notification is sent to both users with the scheduled time

**Error cases:**
- `400` if either `match_id` or `availability_id` is missing
- `400` if no overlap exists on that day
- `404` if the availability slot does not exist
- `409` if the caller is already in an active call
- `410` if the previous call for this match has expired/cancelled/completed

match_id.description: The match_id of the confirmed match between the two users

availability_id.description: The `availability_id` from `GET /api/availability/overlap/{matchId}` — identifies the overlapping recurring time slot

200.description: Call scheduled — both users must use the returned `channelName` and their own `rtcToken` to join the Agora room

400.description: Missing fields, slot not found, or no availability overlap on that day

403.description: Caller is not a participant of this match

409.description: Caller is already in an active call

410.description: Previous call for this match has expired, been cancelled, or completed — schedule a new one

get.summary: Get all my video calls — screen 45 Alerts tab
get.description: Returns all video calls (past and upcoming) that the caller is a participant in, ordered by scheduled_time descending.

200.description: Array of call records including match participants, channel name, status, and scheduled/expiry times

post.summary: Update call status — called by Flutter when call ends
post.description:
Flutter calls this endpoint when a call ends (user hangs up, network error, etc.). Valid transitions: `scheduled → active`, `active → completed`, `active → failed`, `scheduled → cancelled`.

The cron job auto-sets `expired` for calls whose 20-minute join window has passed — Flutter does not need to set this status.

400.description: Missing fields or invalid status value

get.summary: Get the most recent call for a specific match
get.description: Returns the latest call record for a given match. Use this to check if a scheduled call already exists before allowing the user to schedule another one.

200.description: Call record with status, scheduled_time, expires_at, and channel_name

404.description: No call found for this match

post.summary: Post-call feedback — screen 56 (Love it / Not a Match buttons)

200.description: Feedback recorded. not_a_match also unmatches the two users.

get.summary: Per-category compatibility scores computed from questionnaire answers — screen 54

200.description: Array of { category: string, percent: number }

get.summary: Why Do We Match? — reason strings for screens 50-51

200.description: Array of reason strings (high-scoring categories + gap notes)

get.summary: Get all notifications for current user — screen 45

200.description: List of notifications with sender info for like/match types

data.message: Your video call has been scheduled.

get.summary: Get all active Sparklink Club rewards — screen 46 (public)

200.description: List with reward_id, name, description, discount_percent, qr_code_url, expiration_date

get.summary: Get rewards already redeemed by the current user

200.description: Redeemed offers with full reward details

post.summary: Redeem a reward — screen 47 (tap QR code button)

200.description: Redeemed — returns reward details including qr_code_url

post.summary: Report a user for harassment — screen 56 (Report Harasser button)

reported_id.description: user_id of the user being reported

post.summary: Delete account permanently — screen 48 (cascades all user data)

put.summary: Register or refresh device FCM push token
put.description: Call this on app launch (or when FCM refreshes the token) to enable push notifications.

post.description: Flutter app sends the Facebook access token after the user completes Facebook Login. Backend verifies it with the Graph API, then creates or logs in the user.

access_token.description: Short-lived or long-lived Facebook access token from Flutter SDK

200.description: Login successful — returns JWT + user object

post.description: Flutter app sends the Google ID token after the user completes Google Sign-In. Backend verifies it with Google, then creates or logs in the user.

id_token.description: Google ID token from Flutter google_sign_in SDK

200.description: Login successful — returns JWT + user object

401.description: Invalid or expired Google ID token

post.description: Flutter app sends the Apple identity_token after the user completes Sign in with Apple. Backend verifies the JWT against Apple's JWKS, then creates or logs in the user. Apple only returns the user's name and email on the very first sign-in — on subsequent logins these fields may be absent.

identity_token.description: Apple identity token from Flutter sign_in_with_apple SDK

full_name.description: Only present on the very first Apple sign-in. Omit on subsequent logins.

200.description: Login successful — returns JWT + user object

401.description: Invalid or expired Apple identity token

get.summary: Get public profile of any user (pre-match)
get.description: Used when tapping a liked/superliked notification to view who liked you, before a match exists. Returns safe public fields only — no phone, email, or Instagram.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: user_id of the person to view (comes from notification.reference_id)

400.description: Invalid userId or viewing own profile

404.description: User not found or not verified

post.description: Validates the OTP sent to the user email. On success, returns a short-lived reset_token (15 min) to be passed to /auth/reset-password.
Uses the reset_token from /auth/verify-reset-otp to set a new password.

400.description: Passwords do not match or missing fields

get.summary: Privacy Policy
get.description: Returns the full Privacy Policy text. No authentication required. Shown on the signup screen and in Settings → Privacy Policy.

data.title: Privacy Policy
data.content: SparkLink is committed to protecting your personal information...

get.summary: Terms & Conditions
get.description: Returns the full Terms & Conditions text. No authentication required. Shown on the signup screen checkbox and in Settings → Terms & Conditions.

data.title: Terms & Conditions
data.content: Welcome to SparkLink. By using our app you agree to these Terms...

get.summary: About SparkLink
get.description: Returns app description, version, tagline, contact info, and social links. No authentication required. Shown on the About screen in Settings.

data.description: SparkLink is a dating app built around meaningful connections...
